title: agoseris glauca subsp. scorzonerifolia
intro: Agoseris glauca subsp. scorzonerifolia, also known as western hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, sore throats, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its ability to lower fever, reduce inflammation, and promote digestion, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Western hawk's beard can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be utilized for its medicinal purposes.
